_meta.graded_by: Claude Opus 4.7 (under human review by Luc B. Perussault-Diallo)
_meta.notes: Flask is small + well-organised; the structural-index advantage Sense usually shows is muted because grep+Read can answer thoroughly. Baseline edges Sense by ~0.019 — consistent with the bench's premise that flask is one of the easiest repos for a grep-and-read agent.

sense.notes: Names Blueprint at blueprints.py:18 and sansio/blueprints.py:119, BlueprintSetupState at :34, every routes/deferred method, the sansio split. Flags public_re_export at blueprints.py:11 — useful edge case for downstream imports.

Traces register_blueprint → register → add_url_rule with line numbers; explains the rstrip/lstrip slash-collapse idiom and cites the parametrized test that verifies it. Slightly broader cite ranges than baseline.

sense.step_name: Locate test coverage for blueprint registration
sense.notes: 12 named tests with line numbers + 4 supporting files. Strong WHY-link on test_nesting_url_prefixes as 'the canonical test for the line 367-374 composition'.

sense.step_name: Assess adding a runtime-mutable disabled flag to Blueprint
sense.notes: Recommends registration-time gate, 3 distinct files. Adjusted from 0.85 to 0.90 on map_quality per reviewer — both recommendations are valid.

baseline.notes: Comparable map; explicitly cites inherited methods from Scaffold (route@:336, before_request@:460). Doesn't surface as many edge cases as sense.
Same chain as sense, slightly tighter line ranges (:367-374), articulates override precedence ('does not propagate down to nested children except via the composition logic above').

baseline.step_name: Locate test coverage for blueprint registration
baseline.notes: 13 named tests + recommended pytest command + 'incidental Blueprint use (no registration semantics)' annotation. Slightly more complete than sense.

baseline.step_name: Assess adding a runtime-mutable disabled flag to Blueprint
baseline.notes: Recommends dispatch-time gate, 5 distinct files (adds Flask.dispatch_request at :966/:992). Strongest uncertainty surface on either tool: static-file rule cannot be reversed, url_for leakage, view_functions overwrite, the seven merged dicts 'filterable but not removable'.
